name: Gemini 3 Pro
note: Google's frontier reasoning/agentic-coding flagship; sparse MoE, natively multimodal, 1M context; >200k-token prompts bill at $4/$18 per M.

results.notes: AIME 2025, no tools (100% with code execution)
Humanity's Last Exam, no tools (45.8% with search+code exec)
MMMLU, multilingual Q&A [benchmarkSlug corrected during round-5 full-corpus accuracy re-verify: mmlu -> mmmlu, same score]
LiveCodeBench Pro, Elo rating (not a 0-100 pass rate)
CharXiv Reasoning, no tools, information synthesis from complex charts.
FACTS Benchmark Suite (held-out internal grounding, parametric, MM and search retrieval benchmarks).

LMArena (Chatbot Arena) text-leaderboard Elo at launch: 'It tops the LMArena Leaderboard with a breakthrough score of 1501 Elo.' First model to cross 1500 Elo, per Google's official Nov 18 2025 launch post; corroborated independently by contemporaneous press coverage (VentureBeat, apiyi.com news summary).

Domain breakdown of the model card's aggregated 't2-bench' (tau2-bench) tool-use score (85.4%, itself the average of Retail/Airline/Telecom). Google's evals-methodology doc discloses the per-domain split: 'Gemini 3.0 Pro scores 85.3%, 73.0% and 98.0% on these categories respectively' for Retail, Airline, Telecom. The Telecom figure (98.0%) maps cleanly to the existing tau2-bench-telecom taxonomy slug; the aggregate 85.4% average across all three domains was NOT added since no general/average tau2-bench slug exists in the taxonomy.

Domain breakdown of the model card's aggregated τ2-bench score: 'Gemini 3.0 Pro scores 85.3%, 73.0% and 98.0% on these categories respectively' for Retail, Airline, Telecom. [taxonomy-gap backfill: this score was already verified in an earlier research round but had no matching benchmark slug at the time; added once the slug was introduced.]

Domain breakdown of the model card's aggregated τ2-bench score, Airline domain (see tau2-bench-retail note for the same table). [taxonomy-gap backfill: this score was already verified in an earlier research round but had no matching benchmark slug at the time; added once the slug was introduced.]
Fills the missing 'livecodebench' core benchmark; not a duplicate of livecodebench-pro since the two are methodologically different metrics (0-100 pass rate vs Elo).

verificationNotes:
Directly downloaded and read the official Gemini 3 Pro Model Card PDF ('Model Release: November 2025'). Its evaluation table matches every claimed score digit-for-digit: GPQA 91.9, AIME 95.0 (100% with code execution), HLE 37.5 (45.8% with search+code exec), SWE-Bench 76.2, MMMU-Pro 81.0, MMMLU 91.8, LiveCodeBench Pro Elo 2,439. Release date Nov 18, 2025 corroborated by blog.google, CNBC, and InfoQ coverage. Pricing $2/$12 (base tier) and $4/$18 (>200k tokens) confirmed on ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/pricing (via the now-succeeding Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview page, which carries forward identical base-tier pricing). Model card explicitly names it a 'sparse mixture-of-experts (MoE) transformer-based model... with native multimodal support,' confirming archClass/archDisplay directly.

2026-07-16 core-benchmark gap sweep (mmlu, math, humaneval, livecodebench, mmmu): checked Google's official Gemini 3 Pro Model Card PDF and its companion 'Model Evaluation – Approach, Methodology & Results' PDF (deepmind-media/gemini/gemini_3_pro_model_evaluation.pdf), Artificial Analysis (artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/*, artificialanalysis.ai/models/gemini-3-pro), Vals AI (vals.ai/benchmarks/*, vals.ai/models/google_gemini-3-pro-preview), and Epoch AI's model page (epoch.ai/models/gemini-3-pro, ~26 evaluated benchmarks across agents/coding/math-science/multimodal/misc categories). MATH-500 (96.4) and plain LiveCodeBench (86.4) were added from Vals AI's independent leaderboards, both cross-checked against the model's $2/$12 per-M-token pricing to confirm correct attribution (full detail in each result's notes field). MMLU (plain) is a genuine absence: Google's own eval tables report MMMLU (multilingual) instead of plain MMLU; Artificial Analysis has no standalone MMLU page (404, superseded by MMLU-Pro); Vals AI's catalog lists only 'MMLU Pro', no plain MMLU; and Epoch AI does not track it — all consistent with plain MMLU being 'largely saturated at the frontier' and retired by trackers in favor of MMLU-Pro. HumanEval (plain) is likewise absent everywhere checked (Google, Artificial Analysis [404], Vals AI [not in catalog], Epoch AI [not tracked], llm-stats.com [no score]); Vals AI's own LiveCodeBench write-up states outright that 'benchmarks like HumanEval have become saturated,' matching this corpus's 'long-saturated at the frontier' framing for the slug. MMMU (plain) is also absent, with one notable wrinkle: Google reports MMMU-Pro (81.0%, already recorded in this file) rather than base MMMU; Artificial Analysis has no standalone MMMU page (404); and while Vals AI's UI labels one of its benchmark rows simply 'MMMU' (87.51% for this model), that page's own methodology section confirms it is actually run under the full MMMU-Pro protocol ('We adhered closely to the official MMMU Pro evaluation protocol,' 4-option format, ~1,700-question official MMMU-Pro dataset) — so it is a mislabeled MMMU-Pro data point, not evidence for plain MMMU, and was correctly excluded rather than used to fill this gap or duplicate the existing mmmu-pro entry. Epoch AI's multimodal category (VPCT, GeoBench, EnigmaEval) does not include MMMU either. All three omissions (mmlu, humaneval, mmmu) reflect genuine, cross-verified absences rather than guesses.
